hypothesis: If the root-mean-square difference between conservative forces (−∇Ē) and directly predicted forces (ĤFnc) (UΔ) is used as an uncertainty metric for direct-force NNIPs, then UΔ will correlate strongly with the true force error (εdirect) on both equilibrium and adversarially perturbed out-of-distribution structures across Orb and EquiformerV2 models and will provide better identification of high-error predictions than ad-hoc within-family ensemble variance.
